  • Ensure compliance with security standards, governance policies, Azure Policies, and cloud best practices.
  • Troubleshoot Azure infrastructure, networking, access control, and application hosting issues (L2/L3 support).
  • Collaborate with DevOps, Security, Development, and Database teams for project delivery.
  • Maintain technical documentation for cloud architecture, configurations, SOPs, and deployment processes.

Technical Skills Required

  • 4+ years in Azure admin and Data Engineering, proficient in SQL, and familiar with Azure products/services, specializing in Azure's Data ecosystem.
  • Hands-on experience with Azure VMs, VNets, Subnets, NSG, Load Balancers, VPN Gateway, and Storage Accounts.
  • Experience managing Azure SQL Database, backup/restore, geo-replication, failover groups, and disaster recovery.
  • Proficiency in Azure Data Factory for ETL processes, pipeline creation, scheduling, and data integration.
  • Knowledge of Azure Blob Storage, Managed Disks, File Storage, backup retention, and storage optimization.
  • Experience with Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Application Insights, and performance monitoring tools.
  • Understanding of cloud security, governance, Azure Policies, compliance standards, and best practices.
  • Strong networking concepts: DNS, Firewall, VPN, VNet Peering, NSG, Load Balancing.

Why Join Kott Software?

  • Global Impact: Work with agile organizations, Fortune 500 companies, and government agencies.
  • Career Growth: Exposure to advanced project management methodologies and quality processes.
  • Vibrant Culture: Flexible, high-energy environment valuing innovation and proactive contributions.
  • Competitive Rewards: Remuneration based on expertise, not tenure.

Contact

For applications, email your resume to careers@kottsoftware.com.

Job Summary We are seeking a skilled and proactive Cloud Administrator with strong expertise in Microsoft Azure to manage, optimize, and secure our cloud infrastructure. The ideal candidate will be responsible for maintaining high availability, enforcing security policies, reducing operational costs, and ensuring robust backup and recovery strategies.

Key Responsibilities - Cloud Infrastructure Management: Deploy, manage, and monitor Azure-based infrastructure and services. Ensure high availability, scalability, and performance of cloud environments. Perform regular system health checks and capacity planning. - Cost Optimization: Analyze cloud consumption and identify opportunities to reduce operational costs. Implement cost-saving strategies such as rightsizing, reserved instances, and auto-scaling. Monitor budgets and usage through Azure Cost Management tools. - Security & Firewall Management: Configure and manage cloud-based firewalls (e.g., Azure Firewall, NSGs, Application Gateway). Implement and maintain security best practices across Azure services. Monitor and respond to security incidents and vulnerabilities. - Backup & Disaster Recovery: Design and maintain backup strategies using Azure Backup and Recovery Services. Perform regular restoration tests to validate data integrity and recovery readiness. Ensure compliance with data retention and disaster recovery policies. - Access Control & Identity Management: Manage user roles, permissions, and policies using Azure Active Directory (AAD) and RBAC. Enforce least privilege access and governance policies. Handle identity lifecycle management including onboarding/offboarding. - Change Management: Implement structured change management processes for cloud environments. Review, approve, and track infrastructure changes in alignment with IT policies. Maintain documentation of all changes, configurations, and procedures. - Monitoring & Automation: Set up monitoring tools (Azure Monitor, Log Analytics) for performance and alerts. Automate repetitive tasks using PowerShell, Azure CLI, or ARM/Bicep templates. Optimize operational efficiency through scripting and automation.

Required Skills & Qualifications - Strong hands-on experience with Microsoft Azure (IaaS, PaaS services). - Experience with Azure Firewall, NSGs, VPN Gateway, and security best practices. - Knowledge of backup and disaster recovery solutions in Azure. - Proficiency in Azure Active Directory (AAD) and role-based access control (RBAC). - Experience in cost optimization strategies and tools. - Familiarity with change management frameworks (ITIL preferred). - Hands-on experience with monitoring and logging tools. - Scripting knowledge (PowerShell, Azure CLI, or equivalent). - Understanding of networking concepts (VNETs, subnets, DNS, load balancers).

Preferred Qualifications - Azure certifications such as: Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator Associate, Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert. - Experience with infrastructure-as-code (ARM templates, Terraform, or Bicep). - Knowledge of compliance and governance frameworks.

Soft Skills - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills - Excellent communication and documentation abilities - Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment - Proactive mindset with attention to detail

Location: Cochin/Calicut/Coimbatore Experience Required: 4–8 Years Employment Type: Full-Time

Brief Description

We are seeking an experienced IT Security Lead to strengthen our cybersecurity posture and lead security operations across group entities. This role is ideal for a hands-on security professional who can balance operational leadership, incident response, and governance while collaborating with the CISO, IT teams, and business stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

1. Operational Leadership

  • Lead and coordinate daily IT security operations across all group entities.
  • Manage a small team of security analysts/engineers, ensuring continuous monitoring, alert management, and timely incident response.
  • Serve as the main point of contact for security operations between internal teams and the CISO.
  • Automate security operations using SOAR platforms and scripting (e.g., Python, PowerShell) to improve efficiency and response times.
  • Oversee cloud security operations for hybrid/multi-cloud environments, ensuring consistent monitoring and protection.
  • Maintain alignment with IT Service Management frameworks and change management processes.

2. Incident Response & Threat Management

  • Oversee identification, triage, investigation, and resolution of security incidents.
  • Coordinate incident response activities, including communication, escalation, and post-incident reviews.
  • Monitor threat intelligence feeds and proactively mitigate emerging risks.
  • Maintain and test the Incident Response Plan (IRP) through simulation exercises.
  • Address emerging threats like AI-driven attacks, ransomware, and supply chain vulnerabilities.
  • Provide timely incident reporting to management and regulators.

3. Vulnerability & Patch Management

  • Manage the vulnerability management lifecycle (scanning, prioritization, remediation, reporting).
  • Collaborate with infrastructure/application teams for timely patching and system hardening.
  • Include third-party/supply chain risk assessments in vulnerability management.
  • Ensure cloud-native services are included in patching and hardening efforts.

4. Security Tools & Infrastructure

  • Administer and optimize security tools (SIEM, endpoint protection, email security, firewalls, DLP, etc.).
  • Evaluate and implement new security technologies to address evolving threats.
  • Ensure integration between security tools and IT infrastructure for unified monitoring.

5. Collaboration with IT & Business Functions

  • Work with Infrastructure/Applications teams to enforce Group security standards.
  • Act as a security advisor in IT/business projects, aligning with security standards and regulations.
  • Participate in project reviews and solution designs for "security by design" integration.
  • Support fraud prevention through data analytics and security monitoring.
  • Collaborate with legal/compliance teams to embed data privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR).

6. Compliance & Governance Alignment

  • Implement security governance frameworks, policies, and procedures with the CISO.
  • Ensure compliance with FSC/BOM guidelines, DPA, and ISO 27001 standards.
  • Support audits, penetration tests, and regulatory reviews.
  • Maintain security metrics and prepare reports on operational security posture.
  • Oversee third-party risk assessments and supply chain risk integration.

7. Awareness & Collaboration

  • Support user awareness initiatives to promote a security-first culture.
  • Collaborate with IT, Risk, and Compliance teams for security integration in business processes.
  • Provide technical coaching to junior security staff.

Qualifications & Experience

  • Bachelor's degree in Information Security, Computer Science, or related field.
  • Minimum 8–10 years in IT/Cybersecurity, including 3+ years in security operations/leadership.
  • Hands-on experience with security monitoring tools (SIEM, EDR, XDR, IDS/IPS, Firewalls).
  • In-depth knowledge of incident response, network security, and vulnerability management.
  • Familiarity with ISO 27001, NIST, and CIS Controls frameworks.

Preferred Skills

Key Competencies

  • Leadership to motivate small teams effectively.
  • Excellent cross-departmental communication and collaboration.
  • Strategic mindset with hands-on operational capability.
  • High integrity and commitment to confidentiality.
